---
title: "Intro to Epigenomics"
author: "Wade Boohar, updated by Jeanne Revilla"
date: "11/03/24"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
updated: 03/08/26
Milo Gittings
---

```{r setup}
 knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = normalizePath("/home1/gittings/490_cluster/analysis_data"))
```

#Package Download and Data-cleaning
```{r}
if (!require("sesameData", quietly = TRUE))
BiocManager::install("sesameData")

if (!require("sesame", quietly = TRUE))
BiocManager::install("sesame")

if (!require("limma", quietly = TRUE))
BiocManager::install("limma")
```


#Load in all necessary packages
```{r}
library(TCGAbiolinks)
library(sesame)
library(sesameData)
library(limma)
library(ggplot2)
```


```{r}
if (!requireNamespace("remotes", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("remotes")
}
library("remotes")
remotes::install_github("BioinformaticsFMRP/TCGAbiolinks")
library(TCGAbiolinks)
```



#Query, Download, and Prepare the data.
```{r}
query <- GDCquery(project = "TCGA-BRCA",
                  data.category = "DNA Methylation",
                  data.type = "Methylation Beta Value",
                  platform = "Illumina Human Methylation 450",
                  data.format = "Dataframe")
#GDCdownload(query, directory = "GDCdata")     # <-- you will need to run this!
#methylation450 <- GDCprepare(query, directory = "GDCdata")
```
```{r}
#BiocManager::install("GenomicRanges")
methylation450 <- GDCprepare(query)
```


The above data requires a LOT of memory. My recommendation: GDCdownload() and GDCprepare() in separate R sessions.
R requires a lot of memory for both.

```{r}
methylation_clinical <- as.data.frame(methylation450@colData)
betas <- as.data.frame(methylation450@assays@data@listData) #takes a longer time
cpg_sites <- as.data.frame(methylation450@rowRanges@elementMetadata)

column_mask <- ifelse(colnames(methylation_clinical) %in% c('treatments', 'primary_site', 'disease_type','sites_of_involvement'), F, T)

methylation_clinical <- methylation_clinical[,column_mask]
write.csv(methylation_clinical, 'brca_methylation_clinical.csv')


site_mask <- !grepl('-', cpg_sites$gene) & !grepl(';', cpg_sites$gene) & !is.na(cpg_sites$gene) & complete.cases(betas)
betas <- betas[site_mask,]
cpg_sites <- cpg_sites[site_mask,]

write.csv(betas, 'brca_methylation_betas.csv') #takes a longer time
write.csv(cpg_sites, 'brca_cpg_sites.csv')
```

Use the code below if you are loading in your data (i.e. you downloaded the data and closed the session, but you are back to continue working)
```{r}
methylation_clinical <- read.csv('brca_methylation_clinical.csv', row.names = 1)
betas <- read.csv('brca_methylation_betas.csv', row.names=1)
cpg_sites <- read.csv('brca_cpg_sites.csv', row.names = 1)
```


#(1) Naive Differential Methylation
```{r}
#masking out NAs
na_mask <- !is.na(methylation_clinical$age_at_diagnosis)
methylation_clinical <- methylation_clinical[na_mask,]
betas_clean <- betas[,na_mask]

#creating our variable of interest & deciding the base group
methylation_clinical$age_category <- ifelse(methylation_clinical$age_at_diagnosis >= 16425, "old", "young")

#define your comparison and base group
young_mask <- methylation_clinical$age_category == "young"
methylation_clinical$old <- !young_mask #old is comparison, young is base

#fitting linear models using a "target value"
mval <- t(apply(betas_clean, 1, function(x) log2(x/(1-x)))) 
#mvalue is another statistic for methylation, centered at 0 and ranges from -1 to 1
#this step will take a few seconds to run

design <- model.matrix(~ old, data = methylation_clinical)
fit <- lmFit(mval, design)
fit2 <- eBayes(fit)
```

```{r}
#Extracting model into dataframe
dat <- data.frame(foldchange = fit[["coefficients"]][,2], logPvalue =  -log10(p.adjust(fit2[["p.value"]][,2],method='BY')), geneName = cpg_sites$gene)
dat$threshold <- as.factor(abs(dat$foldchange) < 1)

#Visualization
cols <- c("TRUE" = "grey", "FALSE" = "blue")
ggplot(data=dat, aes(x=foldchange, y = logPvalue)) +
  geom_point(alpha=.2, size=0.6) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = cols) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, colour="#990000", linetype="dashed") + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = - 1, colour="#990000", linetype="dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 2, colour = "#990000", linetype="dashed") +
  theme(legend.position="none") +
  xlab("Fold Change") +
  ylab("-log10 p value") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

#Saving results to a .csv file
write.csv(dat, "methylation_results_age.csv")
```

#(2) Direct comparison of methylation status to transcriptional activity
...INSERT DESeq2 Stuff here to generate 'results'...
```{r}
de_results <- read.csv("deSeq_results_age.csv")

rna_clinical <- read.csv("brca_rna_clinical_data.csv", row.names = 1)
rna_genes <- read.csv("brca_rna_gene_data.csv")
rna_counts <- read.csv("brca_rna_count_data.csv", row.names = 1)
```


```{r}
#Extract genes of interest
downregulated <- de_results[(de_results$log2FoldChange < 1), 'gene_name']
hypermethylated <- dat[dat$foldchange > 1.5, 'geneName'] #what cutoff should you use here?
interest_genes <- intersect(downregulated, hypermethylated)
```

```{r}
#Explore your gene of interest
GENE<-"DNAH10"

gene_counts_mask <- rna_genes$gene_name == GENE
gene_betas_mask <- cpg_sites$gene == GENE

#What are we doing here?
rna_clinical_old <- rna_clinical$age_category == "old"
methylation_clinical_old <- methylation_clinical$age_category == "old"

rna_clinical_young <- rna_clinical$age_category == "young"
methylation_clinical_young <- methylation_clinical$age_category == "young"

#What are we doing here?
rna_old <- as.numeric(rna_counts[gene_counts_mask, rna_clinical_old])
methylation_old <- (betas[gene_betas_mask, methylation_clinical_old])

rna_young <- as.numeric(rna_counts[gene_counts_mask, rna_clinical_young])
methylation_young <- (betas[gene_betas_mask, methylation_clinical_young])
```

```{r}
#Show transcription differences
boxplot(rna_young, rna_old, xlab='Group', ylab='Counts', names=c('young', 'old'),
        col=c("blue","red"))
```

```{r}
#Show methylation differences at cpg sites in this gene 
plot(1:dim(methylation_young)[1], apply(methylation_young, 1, mean), col='blue', type='h', lwd=3, ylab='Average Beta', xlab='CpG sites')

lines(1.3:(dim(methylation_old)[1]+0.3), apply(methylation_old, 1, mean), col='red', type='h', lwd=3)

legend("topright",
       legend=c("Young", "Old"),
       col=c("blue", "red"),
       lwd=3,
       cex=0.7)
```
